nodes.name: Olamide
nodes.blurb: The spine of the archive — Bariga upstart, YBNL founder, street-hop architect, label boss raising the next wave.

nodes.name: ID Cabasa
nodes.blurb: Coded Tunes founder and early producer — signed a young Yoruba rapper when the industry still said English.

nodes.name: Yoruba street rap
nodes.blurb: The linguistic and cultural current Cabasa's scene rode — indigenous bars as commercial risk, then blueprint.

nodes.name: Empire
nodes.blurb: YBNL's 2020 joint-venture distribution partner — the deal that opened the roster, including Fireboy DML, to international reach.

nodes.name: Phyno
nodes.blurb: The Igbo-language counterpart across eras — from 2 Kings through UY Scuti, the constant booth partner.

nodes.name: Wizkid
nodes.blurb: From Omo To Shan on Rapsodi to Kai! on the self-titled — two Lagos careers orbiting each other for over a decade.

nodes.name: Reminisce
nodes.blurb: Fellow street-hop architect of the 2010s Lagos circuit — shared stages and the same mainland-to-mainstream fight.

nodes.name: Pheelz
nodes.blurb: Long-time in-house producer and YBNL co-architect — from early Rapsodi sessions to later reunion cuts. Crew, not a signed recording act.

nodes.name: Young Jonn
nodes.blurb: Producer behind Bobo and later alumni reunions — the beatmaker who soundtracked the Street King peak.

nodes.name: Burna Boy
nodes.blurb: Guest on Omo Wobe Anthem (2016) — two futures of Nigerian music sharing a YBNL beat in the Street King years.

nodes.name: Omah Lay
nodes.blurb: Infinity — the reinvention-era co-star who helped prove street-hop's founder could lead with melody.

nodes.name: Wande Coal
nodes.blurb: Afrobeats melody on the Who You Epp? Refix and The Glory sessions — street-hop borrowing pop's shine.

nodes.name: Bnxn
nodes.blurb: On Unruly with the label's second generation — another ear Olamide backed as the founder stepped into elder mode. Co-sign energy, not a YBNL recording contract.

nodes.name: Lil Kesh
nodes.blurb: First major YBNL unveil (April 2014, with Viktoh) — Shoki-era breakout, then left in April 2016 to form YAGI after a two-year deal.

nodes.name: Viktoh
nodes.blurb: Signed alongside Lil Kesh in April 2014 — first-wave YBNL street act; left around late 2016 with Adekunle Gold and Chinko Ekun as Gen-1 contracts wound down.

nodes.name: Chinko Ekun
nodes.blurb: Joined in 2015; first YBNL singles that February. Exited late 2016 with the Gen-1 cohort, later moving to Dek-Niyor Entertainment.

nodes.name: Adekunle Gold
nodes.blurb: Designed YBNL's logo before signing on 5 March 2015 — debut album Gold reached Billboard World Albums before his contract-era exit in 2016.

nodes.name: Xino
nodes.blurb: Mid-2010s YBNL signee from the Gen-1 wave. Public exit friction surfaced in early 2017 when he apologised to Olamide and the YBNL team.

nodes.name: Temmie Ovwasa
nodes.blurb: YBNL's first female signing (April 2016), days after Lil Kesh's exit — discovered via Instagram covers. Exact departure year is soft; listed among former acts.

nodes.name: Davolee
nodes.blurb: Street-hop signing in late 2016 during the post–Gen-1 rebuild. Tenure was quiet commercially; later linked (unconfirmed in places) with other imprints.

nodes.name: Lyta
nodes.blurb: Signed February 2018 with Limerick; breakthrough Time featuring Olamide. Left in May 2019 after a public calendar dispute — said he never had a written YBNL contract.

nodes.name: Limerick
nodes.blurb: Instagram DM signing in February 2018 with Lyta — Pesin featuring Olamide. Later departed; exact exit month is softer than the announce date.

nodes.name: Picazo Rhap
nodes.blurb: Viral freestyle signing in October 2018 — Olamide posted Signed the same week he brought in Yomi Blaze and, hours later, Fireboy.

nodes.name: Yomi Blaze
nodes.blurb: Signed October 2018 off the same viral freestyle wave as Picazo — Yoruba bars from Surulere into the pre–Empire roster rebuild.

nodes.name: Fireboy DML
nodes.blurb: Signed October 2018 — still current. Jealous on YBNL Mafia Family, then Empire-era global reach; the label's longest-running breakout after the founder.

nodes.name: Asake
nodes.blurb: Signed February 2022 after Omo Ope with Olamide — Mr. Money with the Vibe remade the pipeline. Confirmed exit February 2025 via his imprint Giran Republic.
